subject: Biology
title: The Discovery of Cells
description: A quick, practical guide to The Discovery of Cells.

hook.text: In 1665, Robert Hooke peered at cork through a microscope and saw tiny compartments.

buildup.text: He named them 'cells' after the small rooms monks lived in at monasteries.

discovery.text: Two centuries later, scientists proved every living thing is built from cells.

twist.text: A drop of pond water holds thousands of cells, each alive and working.

climax.text: Cell theory became biology's bedrock: all life is cells, all cells come from cells.

punchline.text: One slice of cork launched a whole science.

quiz.question: What did Robert Hooke see when he examined cork in 1665?
